rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes a chain of methodological claims (bi-encoder scoring, margin loss training, evaluation protocol) whose internal consistency warrants checking; e.g., whether the pairwise-preference framing is coherent with the reported MRR/P@1 metrics.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative results (MRR 0.612 vs 0.594 cross-encoder; P@1 0.487 vs 0.471) are directly cited to support the conclusion of 'slightly outperforms'; verifying that the numbers actually warrant those verbal claims is a natural check.
rationale.R1.6: The passage contains pervasive anaphoric repetition ('Our approach for ranking clarification questions' three times in one paragraph; 'For evaluation' to open three consecutive sentences; 'The neural ranker achieves / the BM25 baseline achieves' alternating six times), which is a primary surface-level issue a 'check this' delegation could be targeting.
